title: axe-core Browser Scans, Interaction States, and Manual Accessibility Evidence · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs axe-core browser scans, interaction states, and manual accessibility evidence from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Axe Rule Scope, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review TypeScript team handles case 2202 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove versioned axe scans across interaction states plus triage, manual coverage, and regression evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Axe Rule Scope, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: axe defaults and one URL represent the complete site.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove versioned axe scans across interaction states plus triage, manual coverage, and regression evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version fixtures and state journeys, compare resolved and new findings, and prevent baselines from hiding regressions.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Regressi…, keep Accessibility Regression connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version fixtures and state journeys, compare resolved and new findings, and prevent baselines from hiding regressions.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Regressi…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Accessibility Regression precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
